justification: All 7+ rules identified with correct invariant classifications. Notification rule (BR-8) correctly marked invariant: false. Sanctions rule (BR-6) included as invariant: true. Daily limit timezone ambiguity surfaced as an explicit escalation. 13 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria provided (well above the 4 minimum). The only minor issue is confidence.level at 52, which is below the 60-85 range specified in the rubric — however, the low confidence is well-justified by the partially-verified ambiguity and multiple spec gaps identified, showing calibration awareness rather than carelessness. The agent also split daily limits into separate rules (BR-3/BR-4) with invariant: false for the specific amounts while identifying the existence of tiered limits, which is a sophisticated and correct distinction.

observations.text: All 7 expected rules identified plus an 8th (partially-verified limit split out separately) — comprehensive extraction
Notification rule (BR-8) correctly classified as invariant: false with clear justification
Sanctions screening (BR-6) correctly classified as invariant: true with strong regulatory justification
Daily limit timezone ambiguity explicitly escalated with three concrete options (UTC, local, rolling 24h)
13 acceptance criteria in proper Given/When/Then format, covering boundary conditions like exactly $1,000 and exactly at daily limit
Sophisticated distinction between daily limit amounts as policy (invariant: false) vs the existence of limits as regulatory requirement
Identified the partially-verified ambiguity — BR-1 says KYC must be complete to send, but BR-4 assigns a send limit to partially-verified, creating a contradiction
Edge cases are thorough — sanctions re-screening during settlement, locked account receiving funds, counter reset on successful login
Confidence level 52 is below the rubric's 60-85 range, though the reasoning is defensible given the partially-verified ambiguity
Correctly did not conflate partially-verified and unverified — treated them as distinct tiers with different rules
